samples.comment: I’m surprised that there is no drawstring bag or case for the headphones. I did at the very least expect a reply from a real person.

This was my 1st positive grid experience. I purchased a Spark Live and I liked it so much, I purchased a second one. For whatever reason the second amp worked fine with the app, but the physical knobs on the back of the amp did not work at all. Tried a few troubleshooting steps. (Factory reset, firmware update) no luck. The help desk was very responsive. It was a bummer that I personally experienced a 50% failure rate after enjoying the first amp so much. Again, support was very responsive, we just could not get the second spark live working and I ended up returning it.

AI incorrectly identified the date of purchase. It took 3 communication exchanges to get to the solution, with at least 24 hrs in between responses. No availability of phone or live text support 

Unimpressive customer service.
I was not a fan that my answer was supplied by AI and not a human

Your firmware update process is not optimal for Windows computers. It doesn’t work on my Windows 11 computer and the AI response I received only stated some steps which do not work. I had to find another computer to update the firmware.

The AI person at positive grid told me that he was getting in contact with someone, and that if it got shipped, it would not be able to cancel. Because the stupid AI took so long, it already shipped. I don't know what to do
I have asked for a better Control X in combination with a Spark amp that is reliable. I have now chosen the much cheaper Airstep.

The support I received did not actually solve the issue. The AI response only repeated troubleshooting steps I had already completed and did not provide any new or useful direction. The login problem resolved itself many hours later, which strongly suggests there was a temporary issue on Positive Grid’s side. There was no clear communication about this, and I ended up wasting a significant amount of time trying to fix something that does not appear to have been caused by my local setup. This was a frustrating support experience and it should have been handled much better.
It would be helpful if Ai was informed of the issue at tonecloud server end, so it's assistance would be more accurate, instead of giving a wrong stock answer that would not solve the problem.

straightforward NO to request for a discount on Spark CAB, since I have a lot of equipment (spark 1 & 22, BiasX and BiasFXII).  I've Paid full-price on nearly everything,. I only asked since they already are offering a discount on Spark2, which I think I paid full-price.  

The biggest criticisms I've seen or heard about PositiveGrid is releasing product, interested people pay full price then several months later it is heavily discounted. Now they stopped support for BIASFXII which a lot of people spent a lot of money on.
I just feel that if an Ai agent is going to respond, its response should be much faster than it actually is.
I still am awaiting postage to semd back my debice under warranty
I purchased the Bias X upgrade from Bias FX Elite four days ago. The order shows paid and order status shows "Unfulfilled".

Ai chatbot was relatively quick. I’m curiuos why these don’t respond instantly though. However it was not quite correct and didn’t see that I had purchased the product directly through positive grid and it was on record already just not registered. I submitted an actual picture of the serial number label on the product and it still asked for a picture in the second message. It also did not see that I had missed a space when tying in the serial #. In the end the actual reason the serial number wasn’t registering was some sort of server issue that a human messaged about. Overall the ai service wasn’t very helpful and the issue resolved itself. It was relatively quick though…

The problem with Dropbox backups isn't new. It's been reported on user forums for a long time. It's a great shame for the company that you can't solve it. What's more, you stubbornly refuse to admit the problem exists. You offer partial solutions, claiming they solve the problem. They don't. Spark is a relatively new product on the market, and many people are already complaining—not about the hardware, but about the company's service. It's scary to think what the future will bring. I have very old Boss devices. Everyone still has company support, even though they're no longer in production. Maybe it's worth taking a look.

I don't like your usage of AI for support emails, its also part of the reason why I returned my amp as well with the stupid AI shit. Other than that the actual humans who responded to my emails were great.
Your AI support is only for the basic issues.  It's also repetitive and took days to finally get a human involved.

I think it’s ridiculous that just because you haven’t bought your product new that you don’t have the ability to use free software that goes with it. I can’t get the original owner to respond to my email to remove his ownership from the software so apparently there’s no way I can use the software to its full extent unless I keep pushing the used temporary button. I think this is ridiculous! You should be able to buy a license if you have one of your products that was purchased secondhand. I’m using it, but I have to feel that there’s more I could do with it if I had full access to the software that goes with it.
I don't need an Ai response telling me how thoughtful I was. It's lame.
Plus using AI?

Awesome helpi
No live chat to soak to an advisor on the location of my order
Awesome help and care. I love y’all Heyling hi.

I have an 800 doar piece of equipment that was sold to me as a top of the line studio quality product that I treated well and didnt use very much but now it is outdated and unsupportted I feel like I should have used it when it was new and not taken care of it it still looks new and is barely used
Awesome help and care. I love y’all Heyling hi.

Support were very nice and friendly, and tried their best to help.  However we weren't able to update the firmware using windows 11. I spent a couple of hours of my life trying to update this silly software.
Why does the control X not support Live for looper mode?  That is mindblowing!  I am returning mine.

The AI person at positive grid told me that he was getting in contact with someone, and that if it got shipped, it would not be able to cancel. Because the stupid AI took so long, it already shipped. I don't know what to do

I bought a Spark 40 regerstered it and used it for 6 years now you tell me its not mine.

No interest in doing any more business with you

The person who answered my queries (Heyling, I believe was her name) was very helpful. However, I find it pretty absurd that the amp faces issues on updating the firmware via Windows 11 computers as a whole. That's an incredibly common operating system, and to have a flaw in the software so significant is not acceptable for a premium product like this.

To have the suggested fix be "Try a different computer with a different operating system" is not feasible for most people.

Dear Positive Grid team, unfortunately, the experience was very poor, and I do not wish to hear about what you are unable to do for me. As a result, I have decided not to proceed with any purchase.
I hope you understand and take this feedback seriously.

Sincerely,

Carlos.
Product and software are not what I expected. Customer service attempted to help but offer no solutions.
I purchased the Bias X upgrade from Bias FX Elite four days ago. The order shows paid and order status shows "Unfulfilled".

Since everything told me regarding the firmware update was already tried but still didn't resolve the issue. The software should work flawless regardless which Windows OS. I will use the product with older firmware but what about future updates and the ease of keeping your device up to date

Nice quick response. 
Unfortunately you couldn’t resolve my issue.

Unfortunately,  my question (how can I pay the Jimmy Hendrix set for the Spark 40 other than with Google pay) received no clean answer I could Deal with.
 So no order is placed...

I didn’t receive any real support. I just received a quick answer to the issue so many people are facing with the same feature same product. 

Spark live could be the greatest amp in the market for non-professional musician’s projects, but without that feature it turns into a super expensive average mixer/amplifier with some sound effects. 

Since I cannot afford to buy additional items to work on my projects, it seems I need to sell my Spark live to buy the item that has the feature I need to succeed in my projects. I hope the Technical Support Team work to creat a patch to include this feature cause I would like to have everything in just one product.
Your AI support is only for the basic issues.  It's also repetitive and took days to finally get a human involved.
I submitted a follow up question that hopefully be answered

As is universal in the feedback world, you're asking the wrong question:  "How was this service conversation?"  The correct question is, "How satisfied are you with Positive Grid's handling of this problem?"  I'll answer both questions:  

"How was this service conversation?"   The conversation went ok, and your rep told me everything his script told him to say.  Blah blah blah, whatever.  

"How satisfied are you with Positive Grid's handling of this problem?"  VERY unsatisfied.  You have a know, repeated problem that you have no intention of fixing anytime soon -- the Jimi Hendrix presets don't show up in the app after purchase, and have to be downloaded manually.  I purchased this add-on for the sole purpose of using those presets, and I can't do that without a very inconvenient work-around.  

It's a problem, it's fixable, and I didn't hear one word from your rep that gave me confidence that you care enough about the problem to fix it immediately.  I paid for something that I didn't get, and your best answer is "one of these days, one of these days."  

I've put a lot of money into Spark products in the last two months, but this transaction sours me on the whole deal.
I think it took too much time before a human got invited in the conversation for clarification… straightforward with the human 10/10!
Several solutions were suggested, but none resolved the problem.
The system asked for the same information twice before it would process a replacement and I never got a replacement notification that it was being shipped

Support was good if u are a musician. However the AI used, when I tried to describe I wasn’t a musician, responses that only a musician would understand. Was trying to explain that the sound from the headphones was softened or enhanced. Then it began to mock the fact that I wanted to use positive as close to natural as I could because the enhancement was nothing like my practices. It actually could not understand why I wanted it to at least be close to my Gibson real sound. 
The answer is that if I knew more about music it would have been perfect, I don’t, it wasn’t. Have no complaints, it’s obvious I have little knowledge and should not have purchased th positive grid because of that. A simple question of how I could get that real sometimes shitty practice sound wasn’t answered cause it couldn’t ascertain what I meant. Anyhow your AI couldn’t answer, went on another AI sight. Will try it next wk. Love the gear though. Buying it was my fault. 
I’ll figure it out. Again only complaint I have is my lack of knowledge.
Lack of a solution to update your product using latest Windows 11 system.
Maybe having an actual person handle this type of request would be more beneficial for both parties

First answer, AI-generated, did not give a solution and it actually repeated my question. The second answer still did not give a solution,  but at least told me that someone is working on the solution
